title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston is the most collision-prone city in the U.S. with drivers averaging 3.7 years between accidents.
Worcester and Springfield are among the five cities with highest crash rates, averaging about 5 years between collisions.
Providence, RI ranks 194th, making it one of the top 10 collision-prone cities, with nearly 6 years between accidents.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, from Falmouth, led troopers on a 105 mph wrong-way chase across the Bourne Bridge Thursday.
Deleon faces charges including obstructing an emergency vehicle, speeding, and assaulting an officer; he's held without bail.
The pursuit was terminated due to safety concerns before Deleon was arrested at his home after barricading himself inside.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a juvenile suspect in a lemonade stand robbery on West Ninth Street.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested and charged with armed robbery and firearm possession related to the June 10 incident.
The community, including Mayor Michelle Wu, supported the siblings after the robbery, raising awareness for gun violence prevention.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering leaving the Lakers for a new NBA team to pursue a fifth championship.
Rich Paul mentioned Boston Celtics' Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens as potential draw for James, though not top destination.
Boston Celtics already added Mitchell Robinson and Mike Conley Jr., highlighting their aggressive offseason strategy.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers, calling the move 'disturbing'.
Parish compares the Brown trade to the Dallas Mavericks' controversial decision to trade Luka Doncic to the Lakers.
Parish hopes for a future reconciliation between Jaylen Brown and the Celtics, suggesting Brown deserves jersey retirement honors.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude and appreciation for Jaylen Brown in a farewell Instagram post after Brown's trade.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round and two second-round draft picks.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics' management and criticized Brad Stevens following the unexpected trade.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics and Neemias Queta agree on a four-year, $56 million extension through the 2030-31 season.
Queta's team option for the 2026-27 season, worth $2,667,944, was picked up by the Celtics.
Queta finished fourth in the Most Improved Player voting for the 2025-26 season with 10.2 PPG and 8.4 RPG.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston, established in 1826, celebrates its 200th anniversary as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Originally constructed around 1716, the building housed a Revolutionary War-era newspaper and played a role in early American history.
The restaurant serves 1,000 to 1,500 guests daily and maintains historical menu items like clam chowder, popular since 1834.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4, featuring Chance the Rapper and more, with Storrow Drive closures.
Gillette Stadium will hold the last of Boston's World Cup quarterfinals on July 9, potentially affecting Route 1 traffic.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail kicks off on July 11 with over 60 tall ships, leading a five-day celebration of America's 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House is the oldest continuously operating restaurant in the U.S., attracting many famous visitors.
Six U.S. presidents, including JFK and Obama, have dined at Boston's Union Oyster House.
Celebrities like Al Pacino, Clint Eastwood, and Meryl Streep have frequented Union Oyster House over the years.
